bird.name: Red-necked avocet
bird.summary: The red-necked avocet (Recurvirostra novaehollandiae) also known as the Australian avocet, cobbler, cobbler's awl, and painted lady, is a w…

relations.subject: Red-necked avocet
relations.evidence: The birds have a preference for salt or brackish water and are generally found in shallow wetland areas that are either fresh or salt, or on estuarine mudflats. The species is rare in Tasmania and an occasional vagrant to New Zealand.{{cite book |title=[[A Field Guide to Australian Birds (Slater)|A Field Guide to Australian Birds: Vol.1
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。
